mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals acute labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp, or the signal fails to predict direction during documented recession starts.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST yields a positive 60-day forward return in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month, but only during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during expansion periods, or the predictive power reverses to negative during the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.4% triggers immediate expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, causing rapid equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 50% of high-inflation months, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rates confirms structural labor market weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE yields positive 60-day returns in > 55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter increases in real GDP confirm broad economic growth, supporting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month, exclusively during stable monetary policy environments.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during stable policy periods, or the signal consistently predicts negative returns when policy is stable.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the industrial production index signal strengthening economic momentum, which consistently drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next five trading days regardless of macro regime.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO increases over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested periods.
